input_dna_matches.expected_link: supports Kamenetsky/Rabinovich branch after OCR repair
maternal side of Nakhman through Sura Rabinovich
surname collision/noise; OCR Kaminsky should not create surname branch

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Russian column: 12 марта 1895. Born Nokhum, male, son of Movsha Kamenetsky, Kamenets-Podolsk meshchanin, and wife Sura Rabinovich. Hebrew column: נחום בן משה קאמינעצקי; mother שרה/סורה בת ...
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: authoritative source for Nakhman/Nokhum birth; corrects OCR errors.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: 12 мая 1895; Nokhum Movshevich Kaminsky; female; father Movsha Kaminsky; mother Sara Raskin; place Kamianets-Podilskyi Ukraine.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: low-confidence OCR output with month, surname, sex, mother surname and place-jurisdiction errors.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Marriage of Movsha Kamenetsky, son of Nakhman, to Sura Rabinovich, daughter of Gersh.
Kamenetsky household includes Nakhman, son Movsha age 7; household surname Kamenetsky.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports Kamenetsky hereditary surname; blocks Kaminsky branch.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Nokhum Movshevich Kaminsky female, born May 1895, mother Sara Raskin.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: should be quarantined as OCR-derived error.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Kaminsky may be a separate branch because OCR shows Kaminsky.
Nokhum appears female in OCR, maybe daughter not son.
Mother may be Sara Raskin, not Sura Rabinovich.

embedded_errors.reason: Kamenetsky/Kaminsky OCR confusion creates false surname branch
male child Nokhum imported as female
Rabinovich misread as Raskin and mother replaced
Podolia Governorate replaced with modern Ukraine only
same child created twice due to OCR errors
public tree copied raw OCR errors as facts

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse metric-book image source src_1601 and OCR output src_1602 as separate source layers.
Step 2: Assign src_1601 higher source authority because image/primary record exists.
Step 3: Compare date fields: Russian column 12 марта conflicts with OCR 12 мая; choose March and flag OCR month error.
Step 4: Compare child sex/gender column: primary record says male; OCR says female; choose male.
Step 5: Compare surname readings: Kamenetsky supported by Russian/Hebrew columns, marriage record src_1603 and revision list src_1604; reject Kaminsky surname branch.
Step 6: Compare mother identity: Sura Rabinovich supported by birth image and marriage record; reject Sara Raskin OCR reading.
Step 7: Resolve I3/I7 as same person with repaired canonical identity Nakhman/Nokhum Movshevich Kamenetsky.
Step 8: Preserve OCR raw text as low-confidence derivative alias/evidence, not as fact.
Step 9: Use DNA dna_1601/dna_1602 to support Kamenetsky/Rabinovich branch; downweight dna_1603 Kaminsky surname collision.
Step 10: Output normalized persons, event, citation and OCR error report for retraining.

surname_Kaminsky_branch.reason: OCR misread; primary sources support Kamenetsky

false_claims: Kaminsky is a separate surname branch
modern Ukraine-only place is sufficient for archive routing

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must merge I3 and I7 after OCR repair.
Engine must select March, not May.
Engine must reject Kaminsky surname branch.
Engine must confirm Sura Rabinovich as mother.
Engine must preserve raw OCR as derivative evidence but not as fact.
